title: Public library workshop page
problemStatement: Potential attendees cannot quickly tell whether the free workshop matches their skill level, schedule, and access needs.
audience: Adults deciding whether to attend a free digital skills workshop
userGoal: Know if the workshop is useful, accessible, and worth reserving a seat for.
businessGoal: Increase attendance from first-time visitors and reduce day-of confusion.

contextDetails.label: Decision needed
contextDetails.value: - [ ] Confirm whether learning outcomes or logistics should lead the page.

contextDetails.label: Out of scope
contextDetails.value: Waitlist rules, recurring series pages, and post-event surveys.

contextDetails.label: Contributors
contextDetails.value: Program lead, branch staff, accessibility coordinator

items.title: Workshop value and logistics
items.content: The paired information attendees need before reserving a seat.
annotations: Group outcomes with logistics so the action is based on a complete decision.

children.title: What attendees will learn
children.content: Create a strong password, recognize suspicious links, and set up account recovery options.
annotations: Learning outcomes outrank date/time because they answer relevance first.

children.title: When and where
children.content: Tuesday, June 18, 6:00 PM. Main Library, Room B. Wheelchair accessible entrance on Oak Street.
annotations: Use the user's local branch timezone if this becomes dynamic.

items.title: Reserve a free seat
items.content: Reservation collects name and email only. Walk-ins are welcome if seats remain.
annotations: Mention free status near the action to remove hesitation.
